# Transcrição: 5-Scaling

## [00:00 - 03:02]

All right, everybody's favorite topic, scale. Let's talk about how to scale what's working in your paid traffic campaigns. Let's get into it. All right, guys. So this video is about scaling. So what to do when it is working, right? This is good stuff. Two thumbs up. You can't see me right now, but I'm putting two thumbs up. What to do when your campaign is working and you want more of the same results. This is a really fun video. And you've really have two options with scale. So you can either scale vertically or you could scale horizontally or you can do both at the same time. So vertical scale is pretty simple and it's probably what most of you imagine when you think about scaling a media campaign. Simply increasing the budget. So you're getting good results and you want to increase the budget so that you can get more of the same results. That's vertical scale. Now horizontal scale is taking that ad that's working and showing it to more people. So by increasing your budget, you're going to show your ad to more people in that particular ad set. That's vertical scale. But horizontal scale is creating new ad sets that target different interests or different lookalike audiences so that you can scale that way too. Both of them are good and both of them can be done at the same time. Now when you're scaling vertically, you do not want to increase your budget more than 50% every seven days. So you do not want to increase your budget anymore than 50% every seven days, especially if you are running an evergreen campaign that's applicable to your business for many months to come. So a lot of people maybe they're spending $20 and they're getting good results. Well they'll go in and jack it up to $200 and expect to get the same results. Unfortunately increasing the budget too quickly is going to throw off Facebook's ability to optimize your campaign. So you want to feed it a little bit more and a little bit more and a little bit more so that as you scale you maintain about the same cost for whatever your success metric is. So we recommend not scaling more than 50% every seven days.

## [03:02 - 06:02]

That will keep your campaign healthy. We also do not recommend scaling your ad set past the point of no return. So what do I mean by this? Let me hop into ads manager. So back to these ad sets that we've been using both of them are at $300. I've scaled them a little bit over the past month or so. And we will continue to scale this budget as long as we have the cash flow in the business 50% every seven days. Now remember our success metric here costs per lead. We're trying to keep this under $7. So right now I can keep scaling and I have enough room to continue to increase the budget. But what about when we get to like $6.50 or $6.75 per lead? At that point right before we're about to cross over $7 is what I call the point of no return. So if you are about to reach the threshold, your benchmark for your success metric quit scaling the budget. Don't continue to scale past the point of no return. Just because you're about to hit the point of no return doesn't mean that you have to turn the campaign off either. So if you're about to hit that point of no return, just simply keep the budget at what it is. I have some ad sets that have been running on the same budget that hasn't been touched for months. And that's because it was about to cross over the point of no return. And I left it right there in its sweet spot. Remember that your market is a revolving door. So if you're targeting interest, there are new people coming into that interest every day. So if you reach that sweet spot, that point of no return, just let it run until you cross. And if you cross that $7 mark, clip your budget back down to whatever it was when you were getting the result that you wanted. So a lot about this is scaling until you're about to find the point of no return and then stop increasing the budget. You can no longer scale those particular ad sets vertically when you have reached the point of no return. It's a really important concept to understand when increasing the budget. So that scaling vertically, don't increase more than 50% of your budget every seven days.

## [06:02 - 09:03]

And obviously do not scale past the point of no return. Now when you're scaling horizontally, this is the most effective way to scale as you can literally scale your campaign almost forever. Like I said, remember that your market is a revolving door. So there are constantly new people that are moving to wherever you can sell your product or service, constantly new people that are becoming aware of a problem that they have. There's constantly new people that are becoming interested in a particular topic. Every day, more people become interested in digital marketing. So even if I'm targeting the same interest, it doesn't mean that those audiences aren't changing in terms of the humans that are on the other side. So this is all about leveraging other interests and look-alike audiences so that you can show an ad that's working to more people. So let me give you an example. So as we're scaling these vertically, we're increasing this budget, we could also be duplicating these ads and duplicating these ad sets and creating new ad sets that show the same ad that we know that's working to more people. So this can be done by creating look-alike audiences based off of people who have taken the action that you wanted. So maybe you create a look-alike audience based off of your thank you page or whatever page you're sending traffic to. You can use audience insights. Look has a tool called audience insights to find more interest like the ones that are already working. And here's an example of a campaign that I've scaled horizontally. So you can see a lot of these I've scaled vertically. We have some pretty big daily budgets in here and over time, we've crossed the threshold of no return and I've turned them off. But this campaign was created a few years ago and over time, I've continued to scale horizontally to new audiences. You can see some of them have worked and have reached their threshold quicker than others. But I'm not just increasing the budget on the ad sets that are working, I'm also duplicating the ad sets and showing the ad to more audiences. So for example, if we're, well, let's do this one. So we'll look at who we're targeting here. So this is pretty simple. This interest is landing page. But if I click here, it's actually going to start recommending other interests that are

most like the one that I'm targeting. So I could start to look at these different interests and decide if they would be good interests to target. ## [12:07 - 13:55]

They're obviously a little bit different in terms of the creative and the way that the actual platform works and that's why in this workshop we've actually added two bonus modules. But with scale guys, please do not overthink it. If you have something that's working, pick that success metric that tells you, yes, this is good. Yes, this is working and start to scale vertically with your budget. No more than 50% every seven days until you're about to hit that point of no return and also scale horizontally.
